market.advice: US session closed healthy overnight: SPY holds above its 50-day (+1.4% this week, though -1.7% over the past month as the tape digests June's rally), breadth is solid at 70% of stocks above their 50-day MA, VIX is calm and falling (16.3, 5-day avg 17.1), and only 1 distribution day is on the tape -- no sign of institutional selling. Leadership keeps broadening away from the AI/megacap-tech complex (NVDA, TSLA, MSFT, ORCL, SMCI, NXPI, INTC all screen WATCH/WEAK here, several well below their 50/150-day lines) into money-center and regional banks ahead of Q2 earnings season, REITs, healthcare, consumer staples and AI-power-demand utilities. Geopolitical headlines out of the Middle East (Iran/Israel, Khamenei funeral coverage) are keeping oil-related news flow elevated but haven't dented equity breadth. For the next session: stay long with normal position sizing, lean into the broadening rotation (banks/REITs/utilities/healthcare) rather than chasing megacap tech names that are extended or broken below key moving averages, and avoid adding to anything already more than ~15% above its 50-day into resistance.

picks.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
picks.thesis: Highest-quality setup in the whole scan: a textbook 2-contraction VCP tightening to 3% with the RS line at new highs and EPS +42%/revenue +13% YoY behind it. No independent TV read resolved for BK, so this call rests entirely on the scan's own data -- which is about as clean as it gets.
strengths: Tightest VCP in the universe (3.0%) with RS at new highs -- institutions leading, not following
EPS +42% / revenue +13% YoY backs the technical strength
Only 2.5% off its 52-week high with room to run
risks: Earnings in 13 days -- binary event risk into the print
No TradingView cross-check available for this ticker -- flying on the scan's data alone
watch: Trim into strength ahead of the July 15 print
$147.29 52-week high is the breakout trigger

picks.name: JPMorgan Chase
picks.thesis: Money-center bank leadership continues into earnings season. TradingView's 15-buy/1-sell STRONG_BUY fully confirms our read, and a 5-contraction VCP tightened to 2.3% is a mature, well-formed base.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY (15 buy / 1 sell) -- full agreement with our signal
EPS +17% / revenue +13% YoY, fwd P/E only 14x -- growth at a reasonable price
risks: RSI 68 with just 0.2% left to the 52-week high -- little room before resistance
Earnings in 12 days
watch: Breakout above the 52-week high confirms; trim ahead of the report

picks.name: Citigroup
picks.thesis: Citi's turnaround shows up in the numbers -- EPS +56% YoY, the best of the money-centers -- backed by a tight 3-contraction VCP (2.0%). TV's BUY (11/6/9) is a notch softer than our score, likely reflecting the stock's longer turnaround history versus JPM/BAC.
strengths: EPS +56% / revenue +16% YoY -- best fundamental momentum among the money-centers
Cheapest fwd P/E in the group at 11x
risks: Earnings in 12 days
TV's plain BUY (not STRONG_BUY) is softer than our score implies -- 6 of 20 indicators sell/neutral-leaning
watch: $145.67-area 52-week high is resistance

picks.name: Terreno Realty
picks.thesis: Industrial/logistics REIT with EPS +40% YoY and TradingView's strongest rating in today's REIT group (17 buy / 0 sell) -- full agreement between our score and TV's independent read.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY with zero sell votes (17/0/9)
EPS +40% / revenue +13% YoY funds the distribution growth
risks: Small-cap REIT ($7.1B) -- thinner liquidity than the mega-cap names on this list
Earnings in 35 days, fwd P/E a rich 38.6x
watch: Sitting right at its 52-week high -- watch for a clean breakout vs. a false start

picks.name: Public Storage
picks.thesis: Self-storage REIT with the tightest VCP in the real-estate group (1.9%) after 6 contractions, and TradingView's STRONG_BUY (16/0/10) with zero sell signals confirms the base is ready.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes
EPS +33% YoY on a defensive, recession-resistant business model
risks: Fwd P/E 31x is elevated for a REIT -- priced for continued execution
Revenue growth a modest 3.2% -- earnings growth is coming more from margin/buybacks than top-line

picks.name: Simon Property Group
picks.thesis: Best-in-class mall REIT posting EPS +16%/revenue +19% YoY -- consumer spending at premium retail centers remains resilient. TV's STRONG_BUY (15/1/10) backs the call.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY confirms
Revenue growth (+19.3%) outpacing most REIT peers on this list
risks: Earnings in 40 days -- furthest out on this list, less of a near-term catalyst
Fwd P/E 33.5x prices in continued strength
watch: 0.6% off the 52-week high -- a very shallow pullback, watch for the retest to hold

picks.name: Illumina
picks.thesis: Genomics-sequencing leader at new highs with TV's strongest healthcare read (16/2/8, STRONG_BUY). The technical setup (6-contraction VCP, RS at new highs) is well ahead of the fundamentals, which are decelerating.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY confirms the breakout
RS line at new highs with a 6-contraction, 2.9%-tight VCP

Earnings in 29 days

picks.thesis: The GLP-1 leader keeps compounding -- EPS +170%/revenue +55% YoY is the strongest fundamental print on this entire list -- and TV's STRONG_BUY (15/1/10) agrees.
strengths: EPS +170% / revenue +55% YoY -- best fundamentals on the list
TV STRONG_BUY confirms

picks.thesis: Turnaround story with EPS +63% YoY and the cheapest valuation of any pick on this list (fwd P/E 12.5x). TV's BUY (14/4/8) confirms, if a notch softer than our score.
strengths: EPS +63% YoY -- the earnings turnaround is real
Cheapest fwd P/E (12.5x) on this list -- room for multiple expansion

Earnings in 28 days

picks.name: Align Technology
picks.thesis: Invisalign maker pulled back 10.9% from its high and is basing again (5-contraction VCP, 5.5% tight) with EPS +24% YoY. TV's STRONG_BUY (16/0/10) with zero sell votes is a strong confirming signal on a name that's cheaper (fwd P/E 15x) than most on this list.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes
Cheapest healthcare pick on this list at 14.9x forward earnings

Revenue growth only +6.2%, discretionary consumer spending on ortho work is a swing factor

picks.name: Fortinet
picks.thesis: Cybersecurity leader with EPS +29%/revenue +20% YoY and a tight, 4-contraction VCP. TV's BUY (14/4/8) confirms, though it's a notch below the pure STRONG_BUY names on this list.
strengths: EPS +29% / revenue +20% YoY -- durable double-digit growth
TV BUY confirms
risks: Fwd P/E 45.6x is the richest multiple in the tech group -- priced for continued execution
Earnings in 28 days

picks.name: Applied Materials
picks.thesis: Semi-equipment name down 16.6% from its high but with the tightest VCP on this whole list (2.1%) and the fastest 4-week RS move (+21.9%) -- a name quietly repairing while megacap-tech peers stay broken. TV's BUY (11/5/10) confirms.
strengths: Tightest VCP (2.1%) of any pick on this list
RS +21.9% over 4 weeks -- fastest relative-strength turn here, and it's happening while NVDA/INTC/LRCX/MU all screen WATCH/WEAK
risks: Still 16.6% below its 52-week high -- this is an early-stage repair, not a fresh breakout
Earnings 43 days out -- the furthest catalyst on this list
watch: A push back above the 50-day with volume would confirm the repair is turning into a real uptrend

picks.name: Apple
picks.thesis: Steady mega-cap climber, only 2.1% off its high, with TV's cleanest read of the day (17/0/9, STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes). Base is wider (8.1%) than the tightest names here, reflecting its size rather than weakness.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY with zero sell votes -- broadest agreement of any pick
EPS +22% / revenue +17% YoY -- still growing at scale

Fwd P/E 32x -- priced for continued growth

strengths: 7-contraction VCP shows patient, deliberate accumulation
TV BUY confirms

9.9% off the 52-week high

picks.thesis: A current portfolio holding for several users -- TV's STRONG_BUY (16/0/10) confirms the technical strength, though the -44% EPS growth figure (likely a prior-year one-off charge) is a fundamental red flag worth watching into the print.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes
Revenue growth +15.8% is healthy even with the EPS anomaly

RSI 72.9 is warm

picks.name: Linde
picks.thesis: Industrial-gases leader at new highs with EPS +13%/revenue +8% YoY. No TradingView cross-check resolved for this ticker, so this call rests on the scan's data alone.
strengths: Steady double-digit EPS growth in a defensive, moat-protected business
Only sector representative from Basic Materials -- adds diversification

Fwd P/E 27.7x is a full multiple for the growth rate

picks.name: Cheniere Energy
picks.thesis: LNG exporter down 16.9% from its high with revenue +24% YoY and a cheap fwd P/E (12.6x) -- the only energy name on this list, offering sector diversification amid elevated Middle East headline risk. No TV read resolved.
strengths: Revenue +24% YoY on strong global LNG demand
Cheapest fwd P/E (12.6x) of any pick on this list
risks: No TV cross-check available
16.9% off its 52-week high -- this is a repair trade, not a fresh breakout; geopolitical oil/gas headline risk cuts both ways
watch: Middle East headline flow is a real swing factor here -- both up and down

picks.name: Moderna
picks.thesis: Highest score in the scan (93.0) and TV's STRONG_BUY (16/2/8) agrees, but RSI 86 and a 4-week RS surge of +56.8% make this the most extended name on the entire list. The eye-popping +260% revenue growth is likely an easy comp against a depressed prior-year base, not a sustainable growth rate. WATCH, not chase.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY confirms the momentum is real, not just a scan artifact
Pipeline optionality beyond COVID vaccines (RSV, flu combo shots)
risks: RSI 86 and +56.8% 4-week RS change -- this is a parabolic move, high odds of a sharp mean-reversion
+260% revenue growth almost certainly reflects an easy prior-year comparison, not durable demand
watch: Wait for RSI to cool into the 60s-70s before considering new money; this is a name to watch, not chase, at these levels

picks.name: SiriusXM
picks.thesis: A current portfolio holding -- TV's BUY (15/4/7) is supportive, but RSI 86.5 and the widest VCP on this list (9.6%) mean the base is loose and the stock is stretched. Cheap on paper (fwd P/E 9.0x) but technically extended.
strengths: TV BUY confirms
Cheapest valuation (fwd P/E 9.0x) of any pick on this list
risks: RSI 86.5 is the second-most extended reading on this whole list
9.6%-wide VCP is loose, not a tight institutional base -- higher whipsaw risk
watch: Let this cool off before adding; existing holders should consider trimming into strength

picks.name: GE Aerospace
picks.thesis: Score of 92 and TV's BUY (14/3/9) are both supportive of the AI-and-jet-engine-cycle narrative, but RSI 91 is the single most overbought reading in the entire scan, and EPS growth is actually negative (-1.8%) despite the run. This is a name riding sentiment and revenue growth (+24.7%), not earnings, into a print in 14 days.
strengths: Revenue +24.7% YoY on strong commercial aerospace engine and services demand
TV BUY confirms the broader jet-engine-cycle thesis
risks: RSI 91 -- most extended reading in the whole scan, high odds of a sharp pullback or at minimum a long consolidation
EPS growth actually negative (-1.8%) -- the rally is running on revenue and multiple expansion, not bottom-line growth
Earnings in 14 days into an extremely stretched technical setup
watch: Do not chase here -- wait for either a pullback to the 50-day or a post-earnings reset before considering new money
